title: Croatian Lamb Pilaf
description: A rich and dense Croatian dish featuring lamb, couscous, and cured meat, perfect for special occasions. This gluten-free-friendly recipe is a hearty and flavorful representation of Croatian cuisine.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add lamb pieces and cook until browned, about 5 minutes. Remove from pot and set aside.
Add diced pršut to the pot and cook until crispy, about 3-4 minutes. Remove from pot and set aside with lamb.
Add chopped onion to the pot and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add grated carrot, paprika, salt, and black pepper to the pot. Cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add lamb broth to the pot and bring to a boil.
Add browned lamb and crispy pršut back to the pot, stirring to combine.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 1.5 hours, or until lamb is tender.
About 30 minutes before serving, prepare couscous according to package instructions.
To serve, place cooked couscous on a large platter or individual plates, then spoon the lamb and vegetable mixture over the top.
